items.title: Jim Cramer's top 10 things to watch in the stock market Tuesday
items.description: Is this an SK Hynix market?

items.title: GM beats on earnings, raises guidance amid 'resilient' consumer, pricing
items.description: GM raised several key earnings forecasts after beating Wall Street's second-quarter expectations as its North American operations continue to drive results.

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran and Houthis threaten Saudi Arabia shipping as mediators push 10-day ceasefire
items.description: The latest cycle of strikes comes amid reports that regional mediators have presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire.

items.title: Intel's foundry lands first named customer under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, as Fortinet signs on for security chips
items.description: Fortinet is the first announced corporate customer for Intel's foundry under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, who took over the chipmaker in March 2025.

items.title: Wall Street is selling more rental homes, as buying ban takes effect
items.description: The largest landlords are all net sellers year-to-date, with 3,180 more homes sold than bought since Jan. 1.

items.title: All eyes are on John Healey, the UK's new finance minister. Here’s what’s at stake
items.description: J.P. Morgan boss Jamie Dimon, who backed Healey's predecessor, has waded into the U.K.'s political debate.

items.title: As the U.S.-Iran war heats up again, these parts of the stock market and economy could be affected
items.description: Investors and economists are deliberating what the latest exchange in hostilities will mean for the broader economy.

items.title: Treasury yields are little changed as investors map geopolitical risks
items.description: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — was broadly flat at 4.594%.

items.title: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly, alleging misleading GLP-1 advertising
items.description: Novo asked the court to permanently stop Lilly from running the "deceptive" ads and require the drugmaker to issue corrective advertising.

items.title: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
items.description: Comments from JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon contrast with investors' recent willingness to look past wars, tariffs and other shocks.

items.title: Nebius stock surges as Nvidia discloses 9.3% stake in neocloud
items.description: Nvidia said it would invest $2 billion into Nebius in March.

items.title: Opinion: Burnham is Britain's new prime minister. Now what?
items.description: The U.K.'s new prime minister faces a huge test when it comes to convincing the financial markets.

items.title: Boeing and Airbus prepare for next narrow-body battle – but airlines aren’t pushing for new jets yet
items.description: Boeing and Airbus are preparing their next narrow-body jets, but airlines and investors remain focused on production, deliveries and current aircraft.

items.title: India's largest asset management firm makes a muted market debut after a $1 billion IPO
items.description: SBI Funds Management, whose billion-dollar IPO garnered bids worth $31 billion, made a subdued debut with shares listing at a 7% premium.

items.title: Abu Dhabi approves $6.2 billion gas project to boost UAE supply
items.description: Abu Dhabi's National Oil Company will invest $6.2 billion to develop the Umm Shaif Gas Cap to expand natural gas production and liquefied natural gas exports.

items.title: Samsung Electronics shares rise as robotics move highlights push into physical AI
items.description: Samsung Electronics shares rose as the company set up a robotics division in a push into physical AI.

items.title: Inflation fears are back in the spotlight as the Middle East conflict keeps oil prices elevated, analysts say
items.description: Inflation fears are back in focus amid worries over escalating tensions between the U.S. and Iran in the Middle East conflict which has kept oil prices elevated

items.title: U.S. condemns China's 'dangerous and aggressive' acts after Philippine sailor injured at disputed shoal
items.description: The flare-up came before Rubio is expected to visit Manila for meetings of Southeast Asian foreign ministers, with Chinese diplomat Wang Yi also in attendance.

items.title: Jim Cramer says it's time to look beyond tech as AI uncertainty rattles the market
items.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer says the AI trade has become too unpredictable, making this a better time to put new money into high-quality companies outside of technology.

items.title: Trump imposing 50% tariffs on certain Canadian goods over alleged trade discrimination
items.description: Canada's relationship with the U.S. has frayed amid Trump's tariff-heavy agenda and vocal displeasure with the nations' trilateral trade pact with Mexico.
